meta.name: IEPA Value Matrix
meta.description: Defines the measurement variables and their weighted importance within the Innovative EcoSystems Prosperity Algorithm (IEPA). Weights reflect each variable's relative contribution to its factor, and each factor's contribution to the overall prosperity score.

tier_definitions.Primary: Core IEPA inputs — required for computation. Highest signal fidelity.
tier_definitions.Secondary: Supplementary inputs — used for validation, enrichment, and cross-checking.
tier_definitions.Tertiary: Contextual signals — used for UI enrichment, scenario modeling, and trend analysis.

factor_weights.description: Relative importance of each IEPA factor within the overall algorithm. Normalized from raw factor weight totals (sum = 11.50).

factors.name: FDI Accelerator
factors.description: Measures a region's ability to attract, retain, and deploy foreign direct investment. Highest-weighted factor — capital attraction is the primary engine of ecosystem formation.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.description: Measures coordination quality between government, industry, and academia. Captures governance effectiveness, regulatory quality, and institutional readiness.

factors.name: Innovation Capacity
factors.description: Measures R&D output, digital infrastructure, IP production, and technology adoption. Reflects the depth and quality of a region's knowledge economy.

factors.name: Resilience
factors.description: Measures stability across peace, environment, health, human development, and institutional trust. Foundational baseline — without resilience, prosperity gains are unsustainable.

factors.name: Entrepreneurship Capacity
factors.description: Measures startup formation rates, scale-up pathways, access to capital, and business environment quality. Reflects the entrepreneurial dynamism powering new value creation.

factors.name: Prosperity Outcomes
factors.description: Measures realized prosperity — GDP growth, employment quality, poverty reduction, and scalable company creation. This is the dependent variable the algorithm aims to predict and optimize.

measurement_variables.description: Primary measurement variables within each IEPA factor. These are the core inputs to the algorithm — Secondary and Tertiary indices provide validation and enrichment but Primary indices drive the score.

primary_variables.name: FDI Confidence Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Investor sentiment and forward-looking capital deployment intent

primary_variables.name: World Investment Report
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Global FDI flows, stock, and emerging market investment trends

primary_variables.name: FDI Inflows (% of GDP)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Realized FDI inflow volume as share of economic output

primary_variables.name: UNCTAD FDI Inflows Database
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Cross-validated FDI inflow data at country and sector level

primary_variables.name: Business Climate Assessment
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Enabling environment quality for private sector investment

primary_variables.name: Greenfield FDI Projects
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: New capital expenditure projects (not M&A) — organic investment signal

primary_variables.name: UNCTAD Investment Monitor
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Real-time tracking of cross-border investment announcements

primary_variables.name: fDi Intelligence Report
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Sector-level FDI attractiveness and deal tracking

primary_variables.name: Trade Facilitation Indicators
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Border procedures, documentation, and trade infrastructure quality

primary_variables.name: Sovereign Credit Rating
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Sovereign creditworthiness and macroeconomic risk perception

primary_variables.name: Sovereign Credit Rating
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Cross-validated sovereign risk scoring (paired with Moody's)

primary_variables.name: Article IV Consultation Reports
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Macroeconomic stability, policy quality, and structural reform assessment

primary_variables.name: Government Effectiveness (WGI)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Quality of public service delivery, civil service independence, policy credibility

primary_variables.name: Regulatory Quality (WGI)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Ability to formulate and implement sound regulations enabling private sector development

primary_variables.name: Worldwide Governance Indicators
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Composite governance across 6 dimensions: effectiveness, rule of law, voice, stability, corruption, regulation

primary_variables.name: Global Talent Competitiveness Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Country ability to grow, attract, and retain talent — human capital alignment signal

primary_variables.name: Rule of Law Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Legal system integrity, judicial independence, contract enforcement reliability

primary_variables.name: Global Innovation Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Composite innovation across institutions, human capital, infrastructure, market & business sophistication, knowledge outputs, and creative outputs

primary_variables.name: Global Competitiveness Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Competitive productivity drivers: institutions, infrastructure, ICT adoption, macroeconomic stability, skills, and innovation capability

primary_variables.name: Stanford AI Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: AI research output, investment, policy readiness, and adoption — forward-looking technology leadership signal

primary_variables.name: Science, Technology & Innovation Outlook
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: R&D intensity, STEM workforce, science-industry linkages, and digital transformation policies

primary_variables.name: Patent Statistics Database
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: IP production volume and quality — direct output measure of knowledge creation

primary_variables.name: Digital Development Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Connectivity infrastructure, digital skills, and ICT access — prerequisite for digital economy participation

primary_variables.name: Global Peace Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Societal safety, security levels, and militarization — stability prerequisite for investment

primary_variables.name: Environmental Performance Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Environmental health, ecosystem vitality, and climate policy performance

primary_variables.name: Human Development Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Composite human wellbeing: life expectancy, education attainment, and standard of living

primary_variables.name: Corruption Perceptions Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Perceived public sector corruption — trust and institutional integrity signal

primary_variables.name: World Justice Project Rule of Law Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Constraints on government power, absence of corruption, open government, and access to justice

primary_variables.name: ND-GAIN Country Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Climate vulnerability and adaptive capacity — long-run ecosystem durability signal

primary_variables.name: INFORM Risk Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Humanitarian risk from natural hazards and human crises — systemic fragility measure

primary_variables.name: World Health Statistics
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Population health outcomes and healthcare system capacity

primary_variables.name: Climate Change Performance Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: GHG emissions trajectory, renewable energy adoption, and climate policy ambition

primary_variables.name: Household Income Distribution
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Income inequality and distributional equity — social cohesion and consumption base signal

primary_variables.name: Lancet Disease Burden Report
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Disease burden, disability-adjusted life years, and public health system stress

primary_variables.name: Global Entrepreneurship Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Entrepreneurial attitudes, abilities, and aspirations — system-level capacity for new venture creation

primary_variables.name: Global Startup Ecosystem Report
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Startup output, ecosystem value, funding, talent density, and connectedness

primary_variables.name: Business Ready (B-READY)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Regulatory framework quality and public service efficiency for business lifecycle — start, operate, end

primary_variables.name: Global Startup Ecosystem Report (Extended)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Sectoral ecosystem rankings, performance benchmarks, and emerging market cohort analysis

primary_variables.name: Enterprise Surveys
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Firm-level obstacles to growth: access to finance, regulation, corruption, infrastructure, skills

primary_variables.name: Inclusive Entrepreneurship Ecosystems
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Equity and inclusivity within startup ecosystems — breadth of participation signal

primary_variables.name: World Economic Outlook (GDP)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: GDP growth rate, economic output trajectory, and macroeconomic momentum

primary_variables.name: Global Sustainable Competitiveness Index
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Long-run sustainable competitive advantage: natural capital, social capital, intellectual capital, resource efficiency, governance

primary_variables.name: World Economic Outlook (Extended)
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Cross-validated macroeconomic forecasts: current account, fiscal balance, unemployment, inflation

primary_variables.name: World Employment and Social Outlook
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Employment rates, labor market quality, decent work, and social protection coverage

primary_variables.name: National Accounts Data
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Cross-validated GDP, consumption, investment, and government expenditure data

primary_variables.name: ILOSTAT Labour Statistics
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Granular labor force statistics: participation, wages, hours worked, informal employment share

primary_variables.name: Unicorn Tracker
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Count and valuation of $1B+ private companies — scalable high-value company creation signal

primary_variables.name: Compendium of Productivity Indicators
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Labor and multifactor productivity growth — economic efficiency and long-run growth potential

primary_variables.name: Poverty & Inequality Platform
primary_variables.measurement_dimension: Poverty headcount, Gini coefficient, shared prosperity — inclusive growth quality signal

tier_weight_summary.description: Breakdown of algorithmic weight by tier across all 163 indices. Shows the distribution of scoring power across tier levels.

tiers.role: Drives the score — these indices are required for any IEPA computation run
Enriches and validates — improves accuracy and enables cross-index consistency checks
Contextual layer — powers scenario modeling, narrative UI, and sensitivity analysis

measurement_dimensions.description: Cross-cutting measurement dimensions captured by the IEPA algorithm. Each dimension is measured by one or more indices across multiple factors.

dimensions.algorithm_signal: Measures both foreign and domestic capital deployment — the lifeblood of ecosystem formation
Institutional quality determines whether other factors convert into durable prosperity
Tracks both inputs (R&D, digital infrastructure) and outputs (patents, startups) of innovation
Workforce depth and quality sets the ceiling on what an ecosystem can produce
Prerequisite signal — high instability overrides positive signals in other dimensions
Long-horizon durability signal — ecosystems with poor environmental resilience face compounding decay
Measures velocity and scale of new company creation — the most direct proxy for ecosystem output
Lagging indicator — validates whether upstream factor conditions converted into realized economic gains
Quality of employment (not just quantity) reflects whether prosperity is distributed, not concentrated
Determines whether a market is accessible and connectable to global value chains
